entry.title: Re: How to calculate bonus proration in Variable Pay using a lookup table?
entry.content:
Hi @xavierlegarrec, I tested your suggestion, but I ran into a challenge. When I read the start date from the background elements, it returns the **last salary change effective date** from the compensation data. From what I observed, the system seems to pick the start date based on the **salary field mapping in Employee Central Employee History field mapping**. Because of this, if the last salary change effective date is **01-Jul-2025**, the calculation results in: * **ALCF Start Date:** 01-Jul-2025 * **ALCF End Date:** 31-Dec-2026 (bonus end date) * **Total Days:** 549

This outcome isn’t what we expected.

Actually, as a workaround, I created another column where I check: If **ALCF Start Date < 01-Jan-2026**, assign **01-Jan-2026** (bonus start date); otherwise, keep the **ALCF Start Date**. This gives the correct dates when I test it, but it’s not sustainable since the date would need to be updated manually every year. Is there a way to read the **actual assignment start date** into the **ALCF Start Date** instead?

entry.title: Released API for F-07 / FB01 / F-44 equivalent posting in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am currently migrating a custom ECC ABAP report (Bank Reconciliation Statement)
to SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud using the RAP (RESTful ABAP Programming) framework as
a Managed Business Object with Draft.

In ECC, the report posted FI entries using BDC recordings for the following
transactions:

- F-06 → Incoming Customer Payment
- F-07 → Outgoing Vendor Payment
- FB01 → Manual GL Document Posting
- F-44 → Vendor Special GL Clearing (Indicator K)

Since BDC is NOT permitted in Public Cloud, I have been searching for the
equivalent released APIs.


✅ RESOLVED — F-06:
I found and implemented CCPAPI_CUSTOMER_PAYMENT_SRV (Communication Scenario
SAP_COM_0276) via OData HTTP POST. This works correctly.


❌ STILL BLOCKED — F-07, FB01, F-44:

► F-07 (Outgoing Payment):
- API_PAYMENT_INITIATION_SRV → Scope mismatch. Covers payment orders,
not direct FI document posting
- I_JournalEntryTP EML → Error: "The operation CREATE is not activated
for entity I_JOURNALENTRYTP"

► FB01 (Manual GL Posting):
- Same BAPI blocked as above
- I_JournalEntryTP EML → Same CREATE not activated error
- No generic GL document posting API found on SAP API Business Hub

► F-44 (Vendor Special GL Clearing):
- I_JournalEntryTP EML with SpecialGLIndicator field → Same CREATE
not activated error
- Factoring Clearing SOAP API → FI-CA module only, not applicable for FI-AP
- No AP account clearing API found


My Questions:
─────────────
1. What is the correct released API / RAP Business Object to post an
outgoing payment (F-07 equivalent) in Public Cloud?

2. What is the correct API for posting a manual GL document (FB01 equivalent)
programmatically from a RAP behavior class?

3. What is the correct API for clearing a vendor special GL indicator K item
(F-44 equivalent)?

4. Is I_JournalEntryTP the correct Business Object for all of these? If yes,
why is the CREATE operation not activated and how can it be enabled?

5. Is there a Communication Scenario that needs to be set up for
I_JournalEntryTP to work via EML?


Technical Context:
──────────────────
- Development: RAP Managed BO, ABAP for Cloud (Tier 1)
- Root BO: ZI_FI_BRS_STATEMENT (based on I_JournalEntryItem)
- Action: PostBRSItems inside behavior class zbp_i_fi_brs_statement
- System: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
- Already tried: MODIFY ENTITIES OF I_JournalEntryTP with COMMIT ENTITIES

Any guidance, SAP Notes, KBA references, or working code examples would be
greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Re: 📢 Survey: ABAP Naming Conventions
entry.content:
I really like to hear about option 2. Sounds like it can simplify a lot, especially in regards of legacy code. Also in case you have several development teams, and they want to do different namings, why not? Naming itself is not a problem, problem in following namings (per package) and making code clean. So in other words naming conventions should a helping tool for developers and not a control tool.

Yes, option 1 sounds good too, but it is more like for greenfield projects. What to do with legacy code? Configure in the tool, will be never done because of complexity. 

In ideal word having these two options would be nice. Someone with more strict rools, or new projects can define one global and be happy. For others package level overwrite of the guide will bring a lot of flexibility. But on other side if we have good package structure, why we need central tool for naming rools. We can do on global package.

So I would preffer option 2!

conses mentioned for the point I would even argue:

- No central maintenance, probably much more effort to maintain : maintaining rules close to code (ideally by code/config text) should be simpler than by UI. 
- No central governance : here I would simply put it as plus. Why central? Development teams should decide by themself. Forcing control was never good idea and never really worked.

entry.title: Step-by-Step: Calling S/4HANA Private Cloud Standard OData APIs with OAuth 2.0 from Client Tool
entry.content:
Introduction

When working with S/4HANA On-Premise OData APIs, many developers still rely on Basic Authentication for testing. While it works, OAuth 2.0 is the recommended and more secure approach.

In this blog post, I’ll walk you through the complete process — from verifying your system version to making your first successful API call using OAuth 2.0. 

Prerequisites

  • Administrator access (for OAuth configuration)
  • Any API Client tools. Such as Postman, Bruno (Recommended for localized testing in closed networks)
  • HTTPS enabled on your S/4HANA system

Step 1: Identify Your HTTP(S) URL and Available APIs

1-1. Find Your System’s HTTPS Endpoint

Transaction: SMICM → Menu → Goto → Services

Protocol Port Status

HTTPS443XXActive ✓

Your base URL becomes:

https://<hostname>:<port>

Example:
  https://gclp90XX.devxx.net.sap:443XX

⚠️OAuth 2.0 requires HTTPS. Make sure it’s active.

1-2. Verify OData Service Availability

The quickest way to check if OData services are accessible is to hit the service catalog from your browser:

https://<host>:<port>/sap/opu/odata/IWFND/CATALOGSERVICE;v=2/ServiceCollection?$format=json

If you get a JSON response with a list of services, your OData infrastructure is working. ✓

You can also check specific APIs by accessing their metadata:

https://<host>:<port>/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/$metadata

Step 2: Configure OAuth 2.0 on S/4HANA

2-1. Activate ICF Services

Transaction: SICF

Make sure the following services are activated:

ICF Path Purpose

/sap/bc/sec/oauth2/tokenToken endpoint
/sap/bc/sec/oauth2/authorizeAuthorization endpoint

Right-click → Activate Service

2-2. Prepare a Communication User

Transaction: SU01

In S/4HANA OAuth 2.0 (Client Credentials flow), the Client ID is the User ID and the Client Secret is the User's Password.

  • User ID: Same as Client ID (e.g., TEST_CLIENT)
  • Password: Set this carefully. If you see the error "The passwords specified must be identical", ensure "New Password" and "Repeat Password" match exactly.

 

2-3. Register OAuth 2.0 Client

Transaction: SOAUTH2

  1. Click “Create”
  2. OAuth 2.0 Client ID: TEST_CLIENT (Note: This must match your SU01 User ID)
  3. Grant Types: ☑ Client Credentials
  4. Go to the “Scope” tab → Add the scope for your target OData service.
    Important: Note the exact Scope ID (e.g., ZAPI_BUSINESS_PARTNER).
    You will need this exact string in Step 3.
    ・Create OAuth 2.0 Client

    ・Client Authentication

    ・Grant Type Settings
    Check "Grant Type Client Credentials.."

Step 3: Configure API Client Tool

3-1. Get Access Token (Client Credentials Flow)

In Bruno, go to the “Auth” tab of your request:

TypeOAuth 2.0
Grant TypeClient Credentials
Access Token URLhttps://<host>:<port>/sap/bc/sec/oauth2/token
Client IDTEST_CLIENT
Client Secret<your_SU01_User_password>
Scopeex. ZAPI_BUSINESS_PARTNER (Must match SOAUTH2 exactly)
Client AuthenticationSend as Basic Auth header

Click “Get Access Token”

→ If successful, you’ll receive a Bearer token 🎉

3-2. Troubleshooting Token Errors

If you get "No access token received", use Bruno's Timeline tab or a manual POST request to see the raw error from SAP:

  • invalid_scope: The Scope string is wrong. Check SOAUTH2 for prefixes/suffixes.
  • invalid_client: The Client Secret (Password) or ID is incorrect.
  • SSL Verification: If using a self-signed certificate, go to Bruno Settings → General → SSL/TLS Certificate Verification → OFF.

Step 4: Make Your First API Call

4-1. GET Request (Read Business Partner)

GET https://<host>:<port>/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/A_BusinessPartner?$top=5&$format=json

Headers:
  Authorization: Bearer {{your_access_token}}

Expected response: 200 OK with Business Partner data in JSON ✓

Useful Transactions Reference

Transaction Purpose

SMICMCheck HTTP/HTTPS ports and ICM status
SOAUTH2OAuth 2.0 client configuration
SICFActivate / deactivate ICF services
SU01User management (Set Client Secret here)

Conclusion

Setting up OAuth 2.0 for S/4HANA OData APIs involves several configuration steps, but once done, it provides a much more secure and standards-based authentication mechanism. 

I hope this guide helps you get started with OAuth 2.0 on your S/4HANA landscape!

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition seamless integration with Shopify - Complete Setup and Demo Gui
entry.content:
Great walkthrough, thanks for sharing this detailed setup.

One challenge many teams run into when connecting SAP S4HANA with Shopify is maintaining consistent real-time synchronization across product, inventory, and order flows when transaction volume increases.

In several implementations, we have seen that defining ERP as the system of record for inventory and pricing helps avoid conflicts between storefront updates and backend master data. Monitoring message logs in SAP Integration Suite also becomes critical for catching replication failures early.

The delivery replication step you demonstrated is particularly useful because fulfillment status updates in Shopify often become a pain point when the outbound delivery process is handled entirely in SAP.

Curious to hear if you’ve also explored handling high-volume order spikes or retry mechanisms for failed i Flows in this integration scenario.

entry.content:
The package is designed for SAP S/4HANA Cloud APIs, so its not work directly with an on-premise SAP S/4HANA.

You can still integrate it with BigCommerce, but you will need to adapt the i Flows in SAP Integration Suite and expose the required APIs from your on-prem system.

So it is possible, but it requires custom adjustments to the integration flows.

entry.title: Re: Depreciation Key for Intangible Assets (No 50% reduct...
entry.content:
Good day @Andrea_Gr 

Thank you for your question.

The key point you need to know is, Italian tax depreciation is generally handled through a separate tax depreciation area, not only through one generic depreciation key. The common areas used in standard Italian setup is depreciation areas 1 and 32, which are linked to different ledgers for separate valuation handling.

See the following reference for more insights on Depreciation areas for Italy.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/4603118feda9495892abadbe8f8c3d14/3a5ea5678bdc433c91407f587727bd30.html?locale=en-US

 

Question 1: Is there a specific standard SAP Depreciation Key for Italy designed explicitly for Intangible Assets that calculates the full quota without the 50% first-year reduction?

Answer: I am not aware of a dedicated standard Italy depreciation key that is explicitly described as “for intangible assets without the 50% first year reduction”. However, SAP delivers a standard catalog of depreciation keys by chart of depreciation.

See the following reference for more insights

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/4603118feda9495892abadbe8f8c3d14/d9b75066bdc0452f82efd351add3f653.html?locale=en-US

Considering depreciation keys in Italy are “special” and typically not applicable to IFRS, you may consider creating a custom depreciation key also for IFRS via the process of pre-approved Expert Configuration process via template DPK.

Question 2: If not, what is the best practice or the specific SSCUI / Configuration Activity to adjust the period control method to meet this Italian legal requirement?

Answer: the best practice is to validate the requirement in the Italian tax valuation area and check the depreciation key assignment at asset class level. In Public Cloud, the relevant place to review this is SSCUI 102609. See KBA 3587697 and KBA 3597034 for more insights.

I recommend you:

  1. Confirm the Italian depreciation area setup for the company code and verify whether the tax area is configured as expected. As previously mentioned, it is commonly set up with areas 1 and 32.
  2. In SSCUI 102609, check the depreciation key assigned to the intangible asset class in the Italian tax valuation view.
  3. Create a test for an intangible asset and compare the first year planned depreciation with the delivered standard key candidates available in the Italian chart of depreciation.
  4. If all delivered keys still apply a half year conversion, open an incident with the legal requirement and your test evidence

See the following references for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/8865d153c9684608e10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/4603118feda9495892abadbe8f8c3d14/3a5ea5678bdc433c91407f587727bd30.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/4603118feda9495892abadbe8f8c3d14/d9b75066bdc0452f82efd351add3f653.html?locale=en-US

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Order of the primary key columns in Datasphere Tables
entry.content:
hello,

had the same issue. try first to go with the generated table by DSP. and then check the primary key order in hana. you'll see it's different. it does appear in datasphere as being in the same order, but in the end that's not it.
to order it properly you could either change the order from table definition in hana afterwards, or change the order in dsp view by going to last node and click on edit compound key, there you'll see the actual order of it. you have the option to move up/down keys this way reordering and afterwads deploying you'll solve the issue.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition certifications updated to release 2602
entry.content:
We’re excited to share that all SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition certification exams have now been updated to reflect the features, enhancements, and innovations delivered with the 2602 release.

This update ensures that learners, consultants, and implementation teams continue to validate their skills based on the most current capabilities of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

What This Means for You

1. Exams Aligned with the Latest Release

All learning journeys, courses, practice systems, and system-based exams have been refreshed to align with the 2602 product release. Learners preparing for an exam can expect content that reflects the newest system behavior, UI updates, functional enhancements, and configuration options.

2. Updated Learning Resources

To support learners in their preparation, corresponding SAP Learning content has also been updated. You’ll now find:

  • Revised learning journeys and course material
  • Updated hands-on exercises where applicable
  • New or updated learning system environments reflecting 2602

3. Smooth Transition for Ongoing Learners

If you were already preparing for a certification, you can continue studying without disruption. Updated materials are available immediately, and exam codes remain unchanged unless otherwise communicated in the individual learning journey.

4. Stay Current with SAP Innovations

SAP’s commitment to continuous enablement ensures that certifications stay relevant and reflect the latest release cycles. By certifying on 2602, you demonstrate knowledge and readiness aligned with SAP’s most recent innovations.

What You Should Do Next

Visit the updated learning journeys to access the updated materials:

    • Applying End-to-End Business Processes in SAP Business Suite
    • Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 
    • Implementing Financial Accounting in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
    • Implementing Management Accounting in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
    • Implementing Sales in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
    • Implementing Sourcing and Procurement in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

Schedule your certification exam when you’re ready—you’ll now be tested on 2602 content.

entry.title: 552 5.3.4 – message file too big
entry.content:
You see an error  in SAP Business for design in your email outbound.

552 5.3.4 – message file too big
 

The SMTP error 552 5.3.4 indicates that the email you attempted to send was rejected because its total size exceeded the maximum limit allowed by the receiving mail server. In other words, the server refused your message because the email body + attachments (after encoding) were too large.

What the error means (in simple terms)

  • Your email did not send because it was too large for the receiving server.

  • This may appear as:
    • “552 5.3.4 Message size exceeds fixed limit”
    • “Message rejected. Size exceeds maximum permitted.”

  • The rejection typically comes from the recipient’s server, not your own.

Common causes

  • Large attachments (images, videos, PDFs, etc.)
  • Multiple attachments whose combined size exceeds limits (especially after Base64 encoding, which can add ~33% overhead).

  • Long email threads with accumulated attachments.
  • Server‑specific size limits, such as Gmail’s 25 MB maximum.

How to fix it

  1. Reduce attachment size
    • Compress files (ZIP/RAR).
    • Use lower‑resolution images.

entry.title: Re: Customer Down Payment in SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello @muhammad_yousufali 

Thank you for your question.

I reviewed your requirement and the direct answer is no. In standard process, you cannot directly post a vendor or customer down payment with special G/L indicator A in the Fiori payment and clearing apps without first creating a down payment request.  Special type A G/L transactions can only be used together with a down payment request. See 3113965 - Error "Special G/L transactions of type A are not supported" in Fiori Apps - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me for more details.

This means that apps such as Post Incoming Payments, Post Outgoing Payments, Clear Incoming Payments and Clear Outgoing Payments do not support direct manual use of special G/L type A as a standalone posting flow.

The correct standard process is for you to:

  • Create the request first in Manage Customer Down Payment Requests or Manag Supplier Down Payments Request.
  • Then post the payment against that request.
  • Clear it later against the final invoice if you need to.

A further important point is that you cannot freely create or change special G/L indicator in configuration.

See KBA 2761212,  KBA 3169465  and KBA 3676390 for more insights.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: How To Connect SAP Cloud Connector to SAP BTP
entry.content:
Step2: Connect Cloud Connector to BTP:

1. Open Cloud Connector (https://localhost:8443😞

If you are logging in for the first time, use the SAP standard default credentials:

  • User Name: Administrator
  • Password:  manage

After the first login, you will be prompted to change the password. Set a new secure password before proceeding.

After logging into SAP Cloud Connector:

Scenario 1: First-Time Configuration

If you are configuring Cloud Connector for the first time:

  • Click on “Add Subaccount”
  • Enter your SAP BTP Subaccount details
  • Save the configuration
  • Scenario 2: Subaccount Already Exists
  • If a subaccount was previously configured:
  • Click on “Select Subaccount”
  • Choose the existing subaccount from the list
  • Open the configuration

If you already have an SAP BTP account, after logging into the BTP Cockpit:

  1. Navigate to your Global Account

  2. Select your required Subaccount

For example:   Nayak_BTP_C--- This is My BTP Account 

Select Your BTP Subaccount and click on ok . after that you can  select Cloud to On-Premises Button.

Please ' + ' Button   

Onces Click on + Button We can See and follow the   Below Steps:

 

Click On Next

How to Find  out Host  Name and Port Number:
Go to  SMICM Tode on Your on Premises  System and click on Goto Button Select on  Services.

Please give above details  Hear.


Please Give  Internal Host name and Port Number Then Click On Next Button 

 

Please Give   same as  above  details to Virtual  Host name and Port NumberThen Click On Next Button 

(Internal Host name and Port Number<-->Virtual Host name and Port Number)--- Both  Give Same Details:

Hear System ID Means---> Your On Premises System ID.

Once's Done all  Adding of Details  you can check on below to  Check Availability button, If Everything Is Correct  we can  see   Reachable  Sucessfull Message .If There Is an Issue then  you will get  error Message.

 and Please click on "+"  Button and add to below URL Path it is  Fixed.

  Step           

Once the status shows Reachable, the Cloud Connector setup is successful.

https://community.sap.com/t5/abap-blog-posts/how-to-connect-on-premise-abap-system-to-sap-btp-business-application/ba-p/14340330

Thank You,

entry.title: FM Budget Regime Setup Question (Annual vs Multi-Year Ledgers)
entry.content:
Hello SAP FM Experts,

My client is currently using the SAP FM module with an annual budget regime for both investments and expenses, and for both ledgers: Payment and Commitment.

They are now considering a change in configuration. Specifically, they would like to move to a multi-year regime for investments, but only for the Commitment Ledger, while keeping the Payment Ledger on an annual basis.

Detailed requirement:

Investments

Commitment Ledger: Multi-year

Payment Ledger: Annual

Expenses

Commitment Ledger: Annual

Payment Ledger: Annual

Business need:

The client wants to be able to post a purchase order for the full value of an investment project.
For example, for a 3-year investment project totaling 30,000 EUR, they would like:

The full amount (30,000 EUR) to be committed against the investment budget in the Commitment Ledger (multi-year).

In the Payment Ledger, only the current year's expected invoicing amount (e.g., 10,000 EUR) should be recorded under the annual budget.

I suggested using Fund Types, but the IT team indicated that this configuration would apply to both ledgers, which does not meet the client’s requirement.

Could you please advise if there is a recommended approach or configuration that would allow this setup?

Kind regards,
Rabah

 

 # Public sector  # SAP Funds management  #  funds management, # SAP budgeting and Planning for Public Sector

entry.title: Run Datasphere Task Chains from SAC Multi-Actions via External API Step
entry.content:
Abstract

With the availability of the Task Chain public API, you can now start SAP Datasphere task chains directly from SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) Multi-Actions using the External API step. This enables more automated end-to-end processes that require coordinated actions in both SAC and Datasphere (DSP).

In this article, we cover the prerequisites and detailed setup for starting DSP task chains from SAC Multi-Actions using the External API step. To connect the dots, we also include a simplified seamless planning scenario that demonstrates the integration end to end.

Prerequisites

  • SAP Datasphere provides a public API for Task Chains starting in DSP 2026.02. Reference: https://api.sap.com/api/DatasphereTasks/overview

  • A technical user must already be enabled via the OAuth Client definition in Datasphere. This allows you to create a technical user with specific authorizations to call the public APIs and provides more flexible and controlled security management.

  • The SAC Multi-Actions External API step supports calling the Task Chain API starting in SAC 2026.02 (2026.QRC1).

Configuration

SAP Datasphere (DSP)

1) Prepare the Task Chain

Create a task chain in Datasphere:

task chain defined in DSP

2) Create an OAuth Client

Create an OAuth Client in Datasphere with the following settings:

  • Purpose: Select Technical User

  • Roles: Assign a role that has access to the space where the task chain is located

  • Authorization: Select Client Credentials

oauth client definition in DSP

3) Review the API Definition

Refer to the Task Chain API documentation here:

Overview | Tasks | SAP Business Accelerator Hub

SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)

1) Create a Connection to Datasphere

Create an http api connection to Datasphere which will be leveraged by multi-actions API step:

Create http api connection in SAC

2) Configure a Multi-Action with an External API Step

Because the Task Chain API runs asynchronously, you typically configure:

  • One API call to start the task chain

  • A second API call to poll and retrieve the execution status

Step A: Configuration of Starting the Task Chain

In the Generic API Settings section:

Request fields

  • Method: POST

  • API URL: https://<host name>/api/v1/datasphere/tasks/chains/<dsp space name>/run/<technical name of task chain>

Generic API settings

Response fields

  • Select Get API trigger status from HTTP status code and response body.

response setting

Click Configure Fields Mapping and define the expected response format, for example:

{
  "logId": 123456
}

Map the logId field in response to Job ID:

response fields mapping

Leave the status mapping empty. The API step will use the HTTP status code to determine whether the task chain was started successfully.

Step B: Configuration of Polling the Execution Status

In the Get API Execution Result section:

  • Method: Select Asynchronous Return

  • Polling API URL: https://<host name>/api/v1/datasphere/tasks/logs/<dsp space id>/${jobId}

In Response fields, select Get API execution result from HTTP status code and response body:

query api setting

Click Configure Fields Mapping and define the expected response format, for example:

{
 "status": ""
}

In Fields Mapping, map the status field to Status:

query response fields mapping

In Status Mapping, define the following mappings:

API Step Status ValueExternal Application Status Value
DONECOMPLETED
FAILEDFAILED
IN_PROCESSRUNNING

Run the Multi-Action

Run the Multi-Action. The API step completes when the Task Chain execution finishes:

Task chain monitor in DSP

multi-actions notification

Sample scenario

Below is a mimic example of using a Datasphere task chain in SAC Multi-Actions for a seamless planning workflow:

Users plan Gross Revenue by Customer in SAC, then refer to additional customer attributes in Datasphere to calculate Net Revenue, and finally analyze the results with additional customer attributes via a live connection in SAC story.

  • Plan in SAC: Planners plan Gross Revenue by Date, Customer, Version in SAC.

  • Enrich in Datasphere: Customer attributes such as Segment (e.g., Enterprise/SMB), Industry, and Discount Rate are maintained in Datasphere.

  • Compute in Datasphere: Net Revenue = Gross × (1 − DiscountRate)

  • Report live in SAC: Datasphere exposes the combined data through an analytic model consumed by SAC, enabling analysis such as: “How much of our planned revenue comes from Enterprise vs. SMB?”

Step 1) SAC: Create a Planning Model Stored in Datasphere

In SAC, create a planning model (for example,  GrossRevenuePlanningModel) and select an SAP Datasphere space as the data storage location. This allows storing fact and master data in Datasphere and reduces the need for manual export or replication.

A simplified model could include:

  • Dimensions: Customer, Date, Version

  • Measure: PlannedGrossRevenue

Expose the model’s fact table to the Datasphere space so it can be used for downstream modeling.

select DSP space to locate the SAC planning modelExpose fact table in DSPPlanning data in SACExposed fact table in DSP space

Step 2) Datasphere: Create a Customer Enrichment Table

Create a local table MD_CUSTOMER_INFO with:

  • Customer

  • Segment (Enterprise / SMB)

  • Industry

  • DiscountRate

Enriched customer infomation in DSP

Step 3) Datasphere: Create an Analytic Model for Net Revenue Analysis

Create a target local table T_PLAN_NET_REVENUE with:

  • Date

  • Customer

  • Version

  • Segment

  • GrossRevenue

  • NetRevenue

Then build an analytic model PLAN_NET_REVENUE on top of T_PLAN_NET_REVENUE which can be consumed by SAC live connection.

Step 4) Datasphere: Build a Transformation Flow to Populate T_PLAN_NET_REVENUE

Create a Transformation Flow:

  • Sources:

    1. Exposed SAC plan fact object

    2. MD_CUSTOMER_INFO (local table)

  • Transform: Join and calculate NetRevenue

  • Target: T_PLAN_NET_REVENUE

Minimal SQL logic (inside the Transformation Flow “SQL View Transform”)

SELECT

p."Date_CALMONTH",
​
p."Customer_ID",
​
p."Version_ID",
​
c."Segment",
​
p."PlannedGrossRevenue" AS "GrossRevenue",
​
p."PlannedGrossRevenue" * (1 - COALESCE(c."DiscountRate", 0)) AS "NetRevenue"
​
FROM "sap.sac.GrossRevenuePlanningModel" p
​
LEFT JOIN "MD_CUSTOMER_INFO" c
​
 ON p."Customer_ID" = c."Customer"

transformation flow definition

Step 5) Create a Task Chain and Trigger It from SAC

Create a task chain that includes the Transformation Flow from Step 4. Then call the task chain from SAC using the Multi-Actions External API step (as described in the Configuration section above).

Step 6) SAC: Build a Story to Trigger the Multi-Action and Analyze Live Results

In SAC, add a Multi-Action trigger (starter) that runs the task chain. Then build a live report based on the target analytic model (PLAN_NET_REVENUE) in Datasphere.

Run multi-actions to trigger transformation and analyze live results from DSP

After submitting planned data in SAC and starting the multi-actions, it starts the task chain from multi-actions external API step to start the data transformation flow in DSP. And after it gets done, refresh the story and user can analyze the transformed data with addtional customer infomation and calculated measures via a live connection to DSP in the same story.

"

3-2. Troubleshooting Token Errors

If you get "No access token received", use Bruno's Timeline tab or a manual POST request to see the raw error from SAP:

  • invalid_scope: The Scope string is wrong. Check SOAUTH2 for prefixes/suffixes.
  • invalid_client: The Client Secret (Password) or ID is incorrect.
  • SSL Verification: If using a self-signed certificate, go to Bruno Settings → General → SSL/TLS Certificate Verification → OFF.

"

ZFiori.

entry.title: Re: how to generate recurring entries automatically to fu...
entry.content:
Hello @Cheng_Huang58 

Thank you for your question.

Yes, you can use Recurring Journal Entries to "automate" that in your system.

The recommended design is for you to set up the subsidy amortization as a repeated G/L posting, for example from a deferred income or subsidy liability account to the relevant income account and then schedule the posting job in Schedule General Ledger Jobs for Fund Accounting.

Please note, the main limitation here is that this is a recurring journal entry function, not an automatic asset linked subsidy engine that recalculates itself from the remaining useful life of the asst. So, it is suitable for when the periodic amount is fixed and predictable. If the amount or timing changes, you must adjust the recurring entry setup.

Consult these references for more details

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/114f17567f98692de10000000a4450e5.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/cd189f57e7356d10e10000000a441470.html?locale=en-US

In your system do the following:

  1. In Manage Recurring Journal Entries, create the subsidy amortization entry.
  2. In Schedule General Ledger Jobs, schedule the Post Recurring Journal Entries job.
  3. Maintain your desired Due Date correctly (note that postings after the due date are not created).
  4. Ensure your posting periods are open for the periods to be posted.

See the following references for more insights:

3516666 - Requirements for Posting Day-Wise Recurring Journal Entries - SAP for Me

3022472 - Mandatory field Due Date in the Job Post Recurring Journal Entries - SAP for Me

3035119 - Post Recurring Journal Entries job posting to closed periods. - SAP for Me

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Advance Inter-Company Sales(Make-To-Order)
entry.content:
Advance intercompany does not support make to order. 3366067 - SAP S/4HANA 2023: Restrictions and General Information for Advanced Intercompany Sales.

Unsupported Functions and Scenarios

  • Multisystem
  • Structures (main item or subitems) in the standard sales order:
    • Sales bill of material (BOM) creating subitems
    • Advanced ATP creating subitems
    • Free goods creating subitems
  • Order-related customer invoicing
  • Free of charge items without customer invoice relevance
  • Batch split items without customer invoice relevance
  • Solution orders
  • Scheduling agreements
  • Customer consignment
  • Oil and gas functions
  • Non-stock materials
  • Non-valuated materials
  • Service materials
  • Returnable packaging
  • Stock with a special stock indicator
  • Make-to-order
    • Special stock type “E”
    • Sell from stock with “make-to-order production”
    • With or without variant configuration
  • Sell from project stock
    • Special stock type “Q”
  • Deliveries and delivery items without sales order reference
  • Subsequent outbound delivery split
  • Intercompany customer invoice corrections
  • Third-party direct shipment / drop shipment
  • Catch weight management
  • Commodity management
  • Active ingredient management
  • Last mile distribution for direct distribution
  • Preliminary billing documents
  • Differential invoice
  • Empties management
  • Vehicle management system
  • Trader's and scheduler's workbench (TSW)
  • Liquid and gaseous hydrocarbon product management (HPM)

entry.title: Re: Pass unnamed inbound parameter to a CAP CDS action
entry.content:
No, CDS does not support unnamed action parameters. Every parameter must have a name.

The correct syntax is to give the parameter a name and type it with your custom type:

type Type1 {
  acknowledge : String;
  message     : String;
}

action Action1 (input : Type1) returns String;

The caller sends the payload nested under the parameter name:

POST /your-service/Action1
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "input": {
    "acknowledge": "yes",
    "message": "hello"
  }
}

In your handler you access it via req.data.input:

this.on(Action1, (req) => {
  const { acknowledge, message } = req.data.input;
  return `${acknowledge}: ${message}`;
});

This is consistent with the OData standard, where action parameters are always named. The CAP docs show the same pattern — e.g. action addContact(contact: Contact not null) — a named parameter typed with a structured type.

entry.title: Customizing Notification Templates in SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance (IAG)
entry.content:
Effective and personalized notifications are essential for communicating access‑related events to users, approvers, and security teams. SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance (IAG) provides a flexible notification framework that allows administrators to tailor email content to match organizational communication standards, branding guidelines, and compliance requirements.

This blog explains how to download, understand, and customize the SAP IAG notification templates, and introduces a custom template‑analysis guide to help administrators identify the exact sections they can modify without breaking the JSON structure or dynamic variables. 

1. Downloading the Standard Notification Template in SAP IAG

SAP IAG administrators can download the standard notification template package directly from the application. This export contains all notification workflow templates—access request creation, approval, rejection, provisioning success/failure, privileged access events, and more.

Navigation Path

You can download the standard template by following the path:

Administration → Template Upload → Notification → Standard Template: Download

2. Downloaded File

The downloaded ZIP file contains:

  • A comprehensive JSON file with all SAP‑delivered notification workflow templates.
  • Each template includes:
    • Subject line
    • Recipient structure (to/cc/bcc)
    • HTML body
    • Context‑based variables (e.g., "${context.requestId}, ${context.userName}, ${context.link}")

Editing this JSON directly is technically possible, but time‑consuming and error‑prone due to:

  • Escaped characters,
  • Embedded HTML,
  • Nested workflow metadata,
  • Potential corruption of system variables.

To solve this, here is a guide for IAG Notification Customization, which extracts editable areas from the JSON and highlights them clearly.

3. SAP IAG Notification Template - Customization Guide

This document identifies the sections in your SAP Identity Access Governance (IAG) notification templates where you can safely add custom content. For each template, you will typically customize: Subject, Header/Intro line, Body text (within the HTML <body>), Footer/sign-off, and optional branding (logo). Variables like ${context.*} must be preserved.

Legend: Text in Green Font = recommended area to edit; variables must remain intact.

a. Template: notifyapprover_en

Subject: Access request ${context.requestId} requires your decision

To: ${context.to}

CC: ${context.cc}

BCC: ${context.bcc}

Editable Body (inside <body>):

<!--<h3><img src="logo.png" alt="Logo"></h3>-->
<p>Dear approver,</p>
<p></p>
<p>A new access request ${context.requestId} requires your approval. Please check your <a href="${context.link}">request inbox app</a> (SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance) for the access request ${context.requestId}.</p>
<p>This message is automatically generated and for your information only.  Please do not reply.</p>
<p>Best regards</p>

b. Template: notifyrequestapproved_en

Subject: Privileged access request ${context.requestId} has been successfully approved

To: ${context.to}

CC: ${context.cc}

BCC: ${context.bcc}

Editable Body (inside <body>):

<!--<h3><img src="logo.png" alt="Logo"></h3>-->
<p>Dear ${context.userName},</p>
<p></p>
<p>Privileged access request ${context.requestId} has been successfully approved.
You can check its status in the <a href="${context.link}">Access Request Status</a> app (SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance).</p>
<p>This message is automatically generated and for your information only.</p>
<p>Best regards</p>

c. Template: notifyuserpartialprovisioned_en

Subject: Access Request ${context.requestId} was completed with errors

To: ${context.to}

CC: ${context.cc}

BCC: ${context.bcc}

Editable Body (inside <body>):

<!--<h3><img src="logo.png" alt="Logo"></h3>-->
<p>Dear ${context.userName},</p>
<p></p>
<p>Your access request ${context.requestId} has been completed with errors. For more information, please check its status in the Access Request Status app (SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance) or contact your system administrator.</p>
<p>This message is automatically generated and for your information only.</p>
<p>Best regards</p>

d. Template: notifyrequestcreated_en

Subject: Your access request ${context.requestId} has been successfully submitted

To: ${context.to}

CC: ${context.cc}

BCC: ${context.bcc}

Editable Body (inside <body>):

<!--<h3><img src="logo.png" alt="Logo"></h3>-->
<p>Dear ${context.userName},</p>
<p></p>
<p>Access request ${context.requestId} has been successfully submitted for approval.
You can check its status in the <a href="${context.link}">Access Request Status</a> app (SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance).</p>
<p>This message is automatically generated and for your information only.</p>
<p>Best regards</p>

e. Template: notifyrequestrejected_en

Subject: Your access request ${context.requestId} has been rejected

To: ${context.to}

CC: ${context.cc}

BCC: ${context.bcc}

Editable Body (inside <body>):

<!--<h3><img src="logo.png" alt="Logo"></h3>-->
 <p>Dear ${context.userName},</p>
<p></p>
 <p>The access request ${context.requestId} that you submitted has been rejected. You can check its details in the Access Request Status app (SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance).</p>
<p>This message is automatically generated and for your information only. </p>
<p>Best regards</p>

f. Template: notifyuserprovisioned_en

Subject: Your requested access in request ${context.requestId} has been successfully completed

To: ${context.to}

CC: ${context.cc}

BCC: ${context.bcc}

Editable Body (inside <body>):

<p>Dear ${context.userName},</p>
<p></p>
<p>You have been granted access to the requested application. You can check its details in the Access Request Status app (SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance).</p>
<p>This message is automatically generated and for your information only.</p>
<p>Best regards</p>

g. Template: notifyuserprovisionfailed_en

Subject: Access Request ${context.requestId} failed provisioning

To: ${context.to}

CC: ${context.cc}

BCC: ${context.bcc}

Editable Body (inside <body>):

<!--<h3><img src="logo.png" alt="Logo"></h3>-->
<p>Dear ${context.userName},</p>
<p></p>
<p>Your access request in request ${context.requestId} could not be completed at this time. For more information, please check its status in the Access Request Status app (SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance).</p>
<p>This message is automatically generated and for your information only.</p>
<p>Best regards</p> 

h. Template: notifyrequestcancelled_en

Subject: Your access request ${context.requestId} has been cancelled

To: ${context.to}

CC: ${context.cc}

BCC: ${context.bcc}

Editable Body (inside <body>):

<!--<h3><img src="logo.png" alt="Logo"></h3>-->
 <p>Dear ${context.userName},</p>
<p></p>
 <p>Access request ${context.requestId} has been cancelled by administrator.
You can check its status in the <a href="${context.link}">Access Request Status</a> app (SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance).</p>
<p>This message is automatically generated and for your information only.</p>
<p>Best regards</p>

i. Template: notifypamreview_en

Subject: Review Request ${context.requestId} requires your review

To: ${context.to}

CC: ${context.cc}

BCC: ${context.bcc}

Editable Body (inside <body>):

<!--<h3><img src="logo.png" alt="Logo"></h3>-->
<p>Dear ${context.reviewerName},</p>
<p></p>
 <p>Privileged Access Management session ${context.pamObjectId} started by ${context.executorName} in application ${context.application} was terminated. Please review the logs at your earliest convenience in <a href="${context.link}">Privileged Access Review Inbox</a> application.</p>
<p>This message is automatically generated and for your information only.  Please do not reply.</p>
<p>Best regards</p>

j. Template: notifypamapprove_en

Subject: Privileged Access Request ${context.requestId} requires your approval

To: ${context.to}

CC: ${context.cc}

BCC: ${context.bcc}

Editable Body (inside <body>):

<!--<h3><img src="logo.png" alt="Logo"></h3>-->
<p>Dear ${context.approverName},</p>
<p></p>
 <p>Privileged Access request ${context.requestId} for ${context.pamObjectId} requires your approval. Please make your decision at your earliest convenience in <a href="${context.link}">Privileged Access Inbox</a> application.</p>
<p>This message is automatically generated and for your information only.  Please do not reply.</p>
<p>Best regards</p>

k. Template: notifypamrequestapproved_en

Subject: Privileged access request ${context.requestId} has been successfully approved

To: ${context.to}

CC: ${context.cc}

BCC: ${context.bcc}

Editable Body (inside <body>):

<!--<h3><img src="logo.png" alt="Logo"></h3>-->
<p>Dear ${context.fullName},</p>
<p></p>
<p>Privileged access request ${context.requestId} has been successfully approved.
You can check its status in the <a href="${context.link}">Access Request Status</a> app (SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance).</p>
<p>This message is automatically generated and for your information only.</p>
<p>Best regards</p>

l. Template: notifypamrequestsubmitted_en

Subject: Privileged access request ${context.requestId} has been successfully submitted for approval

To: ${context.to}

CC: ${context.cc}

BCC: ${context.bcc}

Editable Body (inside <body>):

<!--<h3><img src="logo.png" alt="Logo"></h3>-->
<p>Dear ${context.fullName},</p>

You can check its status in the <a href="${context.link}">Access Request Status</a> app (SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance).</p>

<p>Best regards</p>

m. Template: notifypamrequestrejected_en

Subject: Privileged access request ${context.requestId} has been rejected

To: ${context.to}

CC: ${context.cc}

BCC: ${context.bcc}

Editable Body (inside <body>):

<!--<h3><img src="logo.png" alt="Logo"></h3>-->
<p>Dear ${context.fullName},</p>
<p></p>
<p>Privileged access request ${context.requestId} for ${context.pamObjectId} has been rejected.
You can check its status in the <a href="${context.link}">Access Request Status</a> app (SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance).</p>
<p>This message is automatically generated and for your information only.</p>
<p>Best regards</p>

n. Template: notifypamrequestforward_en

Subject: Privileged Access Requests forwarded to you for approving

To: ${context.to}

CC: ${context.cc}

BCC: ${context.bcc}

Editable Body (inside <body>):

<p>Dear ${context.userName},</p>

<p></p>

<p>Privileged access management log requests have been forwarded to you for review: ${context.requestIds}</p>

<p>Please review the logs at your earliest convenience in <a href="${context.link}">Privileged Access Review Inbox</a> application.</p>

<p>This message is automatically generated and for your information only. Please do not reply. </p>
<p>Best regards</p>

o. Template: notifypamrequestcancelled_en

Subject: Privileged access request ${context.requestId} has been cancelled

To: ${context.to}

CC: ${context.cc}

BCC: ${context.bcc}

Editable Body (inside <body>):

<!--<h3><img src="logo.png" alt="Logo"></h3>-->
 <p>Dear ${context.fullName},</p>
<p></p>
<p>Privileged access request ${context.requestId} for ${context.pamObjectId} has been cancelled.
You can check its status in the <a href="${context.link}">Access Request Status</a> app (SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance).</p>
<p>This message is automatically generated and for your information only.</p>
<p>Best regards</p>

Important Tips:

- Keep placeholders like ${context.requestId}, ${context.link}, ${context.userName} unchanged.

- You can add branding (e.g., <img src="logo.png">) by un-commenting the logo line or replacing the image link.

- Maintain HTML structure; avoid removing closing tags.

 

4. Customize and Upload Back to SAP IAG

  • Edit the JSON Using the Guide
    • Open the ZIP → extract the JSON.
    • Refer to highlighted areas in the guide.
    • Update only highlight text in the JSON.
  • Save and Upload
    • Save the updated JSON.
    • Upload the JSON file only. Do not zip it.
    • Navigate to: Administration → Template Upload → Notification → Template Archive → Upload

 

  • Check the Processing Status and Process Log to ensure successful import.

Important Note:

The above guide just showing the areas which can be customized and not the complete JSON script from the standard template. You can identity and focus only on the highlighted text in standard JSON script while updating. Ensure not to change any other content from standard template.

entry.title: Recommended Approach for Publishing CAP Services via Third‑Party API Gateways (WSO2 vs SAP API Manag
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

after checking with SAP Support, I have already confirmed that publishing CAP services through a third‑party API gateway (such as WSO2) is technically supported.

What I still need to clarify, and I hope the community can help, is whether this approach is also recommended, and whether adopting a non‑SAP gateway could mean losing key features normally provided by SAP Integration Suite API Management, which we already have active in our landscape.

Remaining Open Questions

1. Recommendation
Even if supported, does SAP recommend using SAP API Management for external exposure of CAP services, especially from a governance and security point of view?

2. Feature Gaps
If we use a third‑party API gateway, do we risk losing important capabilities such as:

  • OAuth/XSUAA integration and token handling
  • Built‑in security policies provided by SAP API Management
  • Deeper integration with BTP services

3. Best Practices
Is there any official SAP best practice that clearly states when a non‑SAP API gateway is appropriate and when SAP API Management should be preferred?

Any guidance, references, or practical experience would be very helpful.
Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Filter Value help results in Child entity based on pa...
entry.content:
Hi @Shivani_Prabahar ,

Building on @MioYasutake 's answer, here is a concrete example of how to implement this.

As mentioned, LocalDataProperty in @Common.ValueList can only reference fields in the same entity — navigation paths like to_Parent/CompanyCode are not supported in OData V4. So the solution is to denormalize the parent’s field into the child entity.

Step 1 — Add the parent’s field to the child entity:

entity Orders {
  key ID : UUID;
  country : Association to Countries;
  items : Composition of many OrderItems on items.order = $self;
}

entity OrderItems {
  key ID : UUID;
  order : Association to Orders;
  product : Association to Products;
  country_code : String; // denormalized from parent
}

Step 2 — Keep the child’s field in sync with the parent. In your Java handler, copy the value on create/update:

@Before(event = {CqnService.EVENT_CREATE, CqnService.EVENT_UPDATE}, entity = OrderItems_.CDS_NAME)
public void copyParentCountry(List<OrderItems> items) {
  for (OrderItems item : items) {
    // Read parent's country and set it on the child
    String orderId = item.getOrderId();
    if (orderId != null) {
      CqnSelect select = Select.from(Orders_.class)
        .columns(o -> o.country_code())
        .byId(orderId);
      Orders parent = db.run(select).single(Orders.class);
      item.setCountryCode(parent.getCountryCode());
    }
  }
}

Step 3 — Annotate the value help using the now-local field:

annotate OrderItems with {
  product @Common.ValueList: {
    CollectionPath: 'Products',
    Parameters: [
      {
        $Type: 'Common.ValueListParameterInOut',
        LocalDataProperty: product_ID,
        ValueListProperty: 'ID'
      },
      {
        $Type: 'Common.ValueListParameterFilterOnly',
        LocalDataProperty: country_code,
        ValueListProperty: 'country_code'
      },
      {
        $Type: 'Common.ValueListParameterDisplayOnly',
        ValueListProperty: 'name'
      }
    ]
  }
}

The parameter types at a glance:

  • ValueListParameterInOut — Maps the selected value back to the local property (the actual binding).
  • ValueListParameterFilterOnly — Sends the local value as a $filter to the value help entity set, but does not write back. This is what filters the list based on the parent’s country.
  • ValueListParameterDisplayOnly — Shown in the dialog but not mapped.

Optionally, you can hide the denormalized field from the UI using @UI.Hidden so users don’t see or edit it directly:

annotate OrderItems with {
  country_code @UI.Hidden;
}

Hope this helps!

entry.title: Can the system notify managers when a team member receives a low performance rating?
entry.content:
Hello,

I would like to confirm whether SAP SuccessFactors Performance Management supports any standard functionality to trigger a system notification to a manager when a team member receives a low performance rating (for example, a final rating of 1 or 2) in a performance form.

The requirement is straightforward: once the form is completed and the rating is determined to be low, the system should automatically notify the manager so that the appropriate follow‑up actions can be initiated.

entry.title: Re: Log files in Cockpit
entry.content:
Hi,

Accessing the files directly from Cockpit is not possible; however, you can check the name of the files. Normally you should connect to the Core Server of SAP CC to see and monitor the corresponding folders. 

In your case, if I understood correctly you are missing some BITs (in an integrated scenario of CC with CI) and want to find out what happened to them. 

I would propose that you first check the logs of the bulkloader and rater instance (located generally under /usr/sap/<SID>/<InstanceID>/work/log and if you do not see anything suspicious, then increasing the log level of your CC Core server is the next option.

Hope it helps,
Doris
